assumptions: The intragroup dividend has created a recognised intragroup monetary receivable/payable denominated in a foreign currency.
The entities involved have different functional currencies, so the related exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation and can affect consolidated profit or loss.
The entity can meet IFRS 9 inception documentation and hedge effectiveness requirements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge of the intragroup monetary receivable
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is a genuine hedge designation route because IFRS 9 permits a recognised asset or liability to be designated in a fair value hedge, and the intragroup monetary-item exception can make the FX exposure eligible in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of the intragroup monetary receivable
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is a distinct hedge accounting route because IFRS 9 also permits a recognised asset or liability to be designated in a cash flow hedge when the foreign-currency cash flow variability can affect consolidated profit or loss.
